items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-1. Why does the order prescribe standard hand-printed characters for manually prepared flight progress strip entries?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-1 supports the rule that standard hand-printed characters prevent misinterpretation when data is hand printed on flight progress strips.

choices.text: To prevent misinterpretation of hand-printed flight progress strip data.
To replace machine-generated strip formats at all facilities.
To allow altitude entries to be erased and overwritten.
To standardize aircraft callsigns to seven characters.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-1. A handwritten weather entry includes the number zero. What marking is required on weather data to reduce ambiguity?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-1 is paired with the note requiring a slant line through the number zero on all weather data.

choices.text: Use a slant line crossing through the number zero.
Circle the zero only if the aircraft is IFR.
Write weather data only in red pencil.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-2. On an en route FAA Form 7230-19 flight progress strip, which block carries the aircraft identification?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-2 and its corresponding en route data-entry table identify block 3 as the aircraft identification field.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-2. On an en route strip, where is the Mode 3/A beacon code entered when applicable?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-2 maps en route strip blocks. The Mode 3/A beacon code, when applicable, belongs in block 27.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-3. In the ATOP oceanic strip format, which field shows reduced separation flags such as M, R, D/3, or W?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-3 and the ATOP oceanic data-entry table identify field 4 as the reduced separation flags field.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-3. In the ATOP oceanic strip format, what do fields 7 and 8 distinguish?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-3 maps ATOP strip fields. Field 7 is the reported flight level, while field 8 is the cleared flight level.

choices.text: Reported flight level and cleared flight level.
Mach number and true airspeed only.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-4. For a terminal arrival strip, which numbered space carries aircraft identification?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-4 shows the terminal arrival strip layout. The corresponding table identifies space 1 as aircraft identification.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-5 and TBL 2-3-4. On a terminal departure strip, where is the proposed departure time entered?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-5 and TBL 2-3-4 cover terminal departure strips. Proposed departure time is entered in space 6.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-5 and TBL 2-3-4. On a terminal departure strip, what information belongs in space 8?
items.explanation: TBL 2-3-4 maps terminal departure strip entries. Space 8 is the departure airport.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-6 and TBL 2-3-5. On a terminal overflight strip, which space contains the coordination fix?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-6 and TBL 2-3-5 cover terminal overflight strips. Space 6 contains the coordination fix.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-6 and TBL 2-3-5. On a terminal overflight strip, what does the overflight coordination indicator identify?
items.explanation: TBL 2-3-5 notes that the overflight coordination indicator identifies the facility to which flight data has been forwarded.

choices.text: The facility to which flight data has been forwarded.

items.question_text: Use TBL 2-3-9. Which identifier is used for the Vice President aboard an Air Force aircraft?
items.explanation: TBL 2-3-9 lists Vice President and family identifiers. The Air Force Vice President identifier is AF2.

items.question_text: Use TBL 2-3-9. Which identifier is used for the Vice President's family aboard a commercial aircraft?
items.explanation: TBL 2-3-9 lists EXEC2F as the family identifier for the Vice President's family, including the commercial row.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-7 and FIG 2-3-8. What is the operational purpose of the control information symbols?
items.explanation: FIG 2-3-7 and FIG 2-3-8 provide authorized control information symbols used to record clearances, reports, and instructions while keeping control status current.

choices.text: To record clearances, reports, and instructions while keeping control status current.
To replace all plain-language markings in every facility.
To define aircraft identity for military flights.

items.question_text: Use FIG 2-3-7 and FIG 2-3-8. What marking method does paragraph 2-3-10 allow when it will aid understanding of recorded information?
items.explanation: Paragraph 2-3-10, with FIG 2-3-7 and FIG 2-3-8, permits plain language markings when they aid in understanding information.

choices.text: Erasing the symbol and leaving the space blank.
Using local identifiers on interphone circuits.
Replacing the strip with a weather sequence.
